authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.50(b)(122) — Rock Creek (Shasta Co.)
sources.quotedText: (122) Rock Creek (Shasta Co.) from its confluence with Pit River to Rock Creek Falls (about one mile upstream).  Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: 2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (CDFW booklet)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 265494, "Rock Creek", Stream, Shasta Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":265494,"gaz_name":"Rock Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Shasta"}; geometry multipoint, first point (Web Mercator -13548231.627, 5013718.2079) converts to lon -121.705835, lat 41.009332, matching the file's recorded coordinate (-121.7058, 41.0093) to within ~5m — coordinate confirmed.

parameters.note: Rock Creek (Shasta Co.), from its confluence with the Pit River to Rock Creek Falls (about one mile upstream), is closed to all fishing all year. This Rock Creek enters the Pit River a few miles below Lake Britton Dam, near the Pit 3 powerhouse reach.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year, from its confluence with the Pit River to Rock Creek Falls (about one mile upstream)
regulations.verbatimText: from its confluence with Pit River to Rock Creek Falls (about one mile upstream).  Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. This water is closed to all fishing all year, so the license requirement is moot here, but is included for statewide-standard completeness.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
